name: Wiki page naming conventions — project prefixing
description: Prevent filename collision when multiple projects/frameworks share wiki. All pages must be prefixed with project name (except truly abstract concepts).

entities_directory.rule: Always prefix with project/framework name. Never use bare concept names (e.g., messenger-component.md is WRONG, symfony-messenger.md is CORRECT).

patterns.description: Truly abstract concepts that apply across all projects — no prefix needed.
patterns.rule: Only use bare names when concept is genuinely framework-agnostic. If it's a specific implementation (e.g., Messenger Component), prefix with project name: symfony-messenger-component.md

patterns.description: Concepts tied to a specific project/framework — always prefix.
patterns.rule: Always prefix with project name. Use hyphenated lowercase kebab-case.

abstract_concepts.reason: Clean architecture pattern (ports-and-adapters), universal across frameworks

framework_exceptions.reason: Doctrine ORM works with any PHP framework, not Symfony-specific

detection_logic.rule: If tags contain project-name (e.g., symfony-messaging) AND filename lacks project prefix → VIOLATION
detection_logic.exception_check: Is file in exceptions/abstract_concepts or framework_exceptions? If yes → ALLOW. If no → ENFORCE_PREFIX

detection_logic.rule: If related contains entities/<project>.md AND filename lacks project prefix → VIOLATION

auto_auditing.description: scripts/filename-audit.sh scans wiki/concepts/ for violations using detection_logic rules

docs_directory.rule: Always prefix with project name. Never use bare concept names in docs/.

comparisons_directory.rule: Compare two entities: both names must be present in the filename.

aliases_system.primary_name_rule: Choose most common/official name as primary filename. Use less common variants in aliases frontmatter field.
aliases_system.rule: Aliases stored in YAML frontmatter `aliases: []` array — never create separate files for alternative names.

injection_prevention.rule: sanitize file names with validate-path.sh before writing.
